title: Andrej Karpathy Discourse - by Elle Michelle Yang
excerpts:
Section Title: Andrej Karpathy Discourse > ... > **Things I’ve consumed** > **Andrej Karpathy**
Content:
Throughout the discussion on learning intelligence, a concept that Andrej kept returning to was “entropy”. I wish Dwarkesh would’ve pushed deeper on the topic, since it sounded like a core mental framework for Andrej. In physics, [entropy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entropy) is a measure of the disorder or randomness in a system, although I want to avoid making [physical analogies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_law_of_thermodynamics) . In modern LLMs, entropy applies to many distinct parts of the modeling pipeline, but can be useful in addressing “intelligence”. Specifically, entropy is a property of probability distributions, which is how we currently model token predictions, which represent the “knowledge” or “intelligence” within an LLM. I interject that no one knows the answer to the “optimal” entropy, if such a concept even exists.

Learning & Documentation
Simulates the expertise and teaching style of Andrej Karpathy to guide deep learning implementation, LLM understanding, and first-principles AI engineering.
About SKILL.md FAQ
This skill transforms Claude into a specialized agent embodying the knowledge and pedagogical approach of Andrej Karpathy, former Director of AI at Tesla and co-founder of OpenAI. It provides deep technical insights into neural networks, Software 2.0 paradigms, and LLM architectures, focusing on building from scratch to ensure true understanding. Whether you are debugging backpropagation, exploring scaling laws, or adopting the latest 'vibe coding' trends, this skill offers clarity, technical precision, and the unique perspective of one of the world's leading AI educators.

title: - Focused on deep learning, computer vision, and natural language processing. He worked on convolutional and recurrent neural networks and was the architect of Stanford’s first deep learning course, CS231n.
excerpts: **Bio** . I am the Sr. Director of AI at Tesla, where I lead the team responsible for all neural networks on the Autopilot. Previously, I was a Research Scientist at [OpenAI](http://openai.com) working on Deep Learning in Computer Vision, Generative Modeling and Reinforcement Learning. I received my PhD from Stanford, where I worked with [Fei-Fei Li](http://vision.stanford.edu) on Convolutional/Recurrent Neural Network architectures and their applications in Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing and their intersection. Over the course of my PhD I squeezed in two internships at Google where I worked on large-scale feature learning over YouTube videos, and in 2015 I interned at DeepMind on the Deep Reinforcement Learning team. Together with Fei-Fei, I designed and was the primary instructor for a new Stanford class on [Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition (CS231n)](http://cs231n.stanford.edu/) .
